author: lOrm
content:
Deferred — architectural test-infra issue rooted in production code (SCHEDULES_FILE is a relative path).

**Fix outline:** The real fix is to remove the relative-path dependency from ScheduleStore::load/save in src/tools/cron.rs — accept the path as a constructor argument (ScheduleStore::new(path: PathBuf)) rather than reading a global SCHEDULES_FILE relative to cwd. Tests then pass a tmp.path().join('schedules.json') without ever calling std::env::set_current_dir, eliminating the entire cwd_lock workaround at src/tools/cron.rs:567-577 + the 5 set_current_dir sites at lines 703/707/736/739/etc. There is a separate issue tracking the SCHEDULES_FILE-being-relative root cause; this one is its test-side symptom.

**Partial progress:** A shared process_cwd_lock (src/tools/testutil::process_cwd_lock) was introduced so cron tests and worktree tests now share a single mutex rather than racing. That contained the symptom but did not address the cause.

**Recommendation:** Bundle with the SCHEDULES_FILE-relative-path fix. Defer.
